papers_mined.title: Taonga pūoro (Wikipedia)
papers_mined.note: Maori 'singing treasures' wind/breath instruments including kōauau, nguru, putorino, putatara; designed for microtonal play; functioned in ritual + healing + spirit communication.

papers_mined.title: Maori musical instruments taonga puoro (Te Ara)
papers_mined.note: Early Maori musicians used non-Western intervals — microtones; instruments perfectly designed to produce microtones outside semitone grid.

papers_mined.title: Maori musical instruments (Te Papa)
papers_mined.note: Kōauau ductless flute 10-20cm with 3-6 finger holes; tonal range subtle; depth of microtonal control.

papers_mined.title: Taonga pūoro Richard Nunns archive
papers_mined.note: Richard Nunns revival research; instrument tonal sub-structure dependent on breath + finger pressure for microtonal expression.

papers_mined.title: Spark-TTS LLM-Based TTS Single-Stream Decoupled Speech Tokens (2025/03)
papers_mined.note: LLM-side: attribute tokenizer with fine-grained values + BiCodec decomposing speech into semantic + global tokens; precise pitch control via fine-grained quantization.

papers_mined.title: SAC Neural Speech Codec Semantic-Acoustic Dual-Stream Quantization (2510.16841)
papers_mined.note: LLM-side: dual-stream codec with semantic + fine-grained acoustic representations.

papers_mined.title: VoxCPM Tokenizer-Free TTS Context-Aware Speech (2509.24650)
papers_mined.note: LLM-side: residual acoustic LM reconstructing subtle vocal characteristics conventional discrete methods sacrifice.

papers_mined.title: CosyVoice 2 Scalable Streaming Speech Synthesis LLM
papers_mined.note: LLM-side: finite scalar quantization replaces VQ for finer-grained pitch/emotion control.
